title: [macOS] Clicking another app from Windows client does not activate or raise its window; mobile client works
author: xiehaibin18
body:
### Bug Description

When controlling a Mac from the official RustDesk Windows client, clicking a window that belongs to another application does not fully activate that application.

The click itself is delivered and the target window remains interactive, but:

- the active application name in the top-left macOS menu bar does not change;
- the clicked application's window is not raised to the front;
- the previous application's visible active state/window ordering remains.

Clicking the target application's menu bar or its Dock icon activates and raises it correctly.

The same Mac controlled by the official RustDesk mobile client does **not** have this problem. A simultaneous A/B test with the Windows and mobile clients connected to the same Mac reproduced the difference consistently.

### How to Reproduce

1. On macOS, open two or more applications with overlapping windows (for example, Chrome and an editor).
2. Connect from the official RustDesk 1.4.9 Windows client.
3. Click inside the background application's window.
4. Observe that the click is delivered and interaction works, but the macOS active application name and window z-order do not switch.
5. Click the same application's macOS menu bar or Dock icon; it then activates normally.
6. Connect to the same Mac from the official RustDesk mobile client and repeat the test; normal activation and window raising occur.

### Expected Behavior

Clicking a window belonging to another application should activate that application, update the macOS menu bar application name, and raise the clicked window, regardless of whether the controlling client is Windows or mobile.

### Operating system(s) on local (controlling) side and remote (controlled) side

Windows x86_64 (exact Windows release/build not captured) -> macOS 27 beta, build 26A5378j

### RustDesk Version(s) on local (controlling) side and remote (controlled) side

Official RustDesk 1.4.9 -> official RustDesk 1.4.9

Windows client build date shown in About: 2026-07-06 10:42.

### Screenshots

The Windows About dialog confirms the official RustDesk 1.4.9 build. A still screenshot does not show the focus/z-order transition failure; a screen recording can be provided if needed.

### Additional Context

The following causes were tested and did not change the behavior:

- AltTab and DockDoor were stopped individually and restored afterward.
- The RustDesk service on the Mac was safely restarted and verified.
- Modifier-key state was cleared.
- A live macOS CGEvent tap showed Windows-client left clicks without Command, Option, Control, or Shift flags (flags were `0x20000000`).
- Mouse movement was absolute, not relative.
- Switching the video codec from Auto/AV1 to VP8 did not help.

A simultaneous Windows/mobile comparison produced different WindowServer activation paths:

- Mobile-client clicks appeared as `PostFilteredEventTapData` and activated/raised applications normally.
- Windows-client clicks caused the Dock universal connection to call `SetFrontProcess`. WindowServer recorded the front process update, but the visible menu bar application state and window ordering did not complete correctly.

The Dock connection was confirmed from WindowServer connection logs; this was not DockDoor or AltTab.

Potentially related issues were reviewed, including #4367 and #14951, but neither describes this exact Windows-client-only activation/z-order behavior.

title: Scale Stretch view mode — eliminate black bars with independent X/Y scaling
author: Kauthar728
body:
## Summary

Request for a **"Scale stretch"** view mode that fills the entire client window by scaling the remote image independently on X and Y axes, eliminating the black bars that appear in **Scale adaptive** mode when the client and host have different aspect ratios.

This was previously requested in #2423 (closed but never implemented) and #4649 (closed). Users are still asking for this in 2026.

## Problem

**Scale adaptive** uses a uniform scale factor (`min(width/displayWidth, height/displayHeight)`) to maintain aspect ratio. This leaves black bars on the sides or top/bottom when the client window aspect ratio doesn't match the remote display.

Example:
- Remote: 1920x1080 (16:9)
- Client window: 1200x900 (4:3)
- Adaptive scale: `min(1200/1920, 900/1080) = min(0.625, 0.833) = 0.625`
- Result: 1200x675 image with 112px black bars top and bottom

## Proposed Solution

Add a `kRemoteViewStyleStretch` view style that:
- Computes `scaleX = clientWidth / displayWidth` and `scaleY = clientHeight / displayHeight` independently
- Calls `canvas.scale(sx, sy)` instead of `canvas.scale(scale, scale)` in `ImagePainter`
- Sets canvas offset to `(0, 0)` -- no centering needed since it fills the window
- Marks overflow as `false` -- stretch always fills the canvas

### Implementation (working proof of concept)

I have a working implementation on a fork of 1.4.9. Files modified:

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `flutter/lib/consts.dart` | Add `kRemoteViewStyleStretch = 'stretch'` |
| `flutter/lib/common/widgets/toolbar.dart` | Add "Scale stretch" radio menu option |
| `flutter/lib/models/model.dart` | `ViewStyle.scaleX`/`scaleY` getters, `CanvasModel._scaleX`/`_scaleY`, stretch offset/overflow logic |
| `flutter/lib/desktop/pages/remote_page.dart` | `ImagePaint` and `CursorPaint` use `scaleX`/`scaleY` in stretch mode |
| `flutter/lib/models/input_model.dart` | Mouse coordinate mapping uses independent `sx`/`sy` |
| `flutter/lib/utils/image.dart` | `ImagePainter` optional `scaleX`/`scaleY` params |
| `src/lang/template.rs` | `("Scale stretch", "")` i18n key |

### Key implementation details

1. **`ImagePainter`** accepts optional `scaleX`/`scaleY` and calls `canvas.scale(scaleX ?? scale, scaleY ?? scale)` instead of `canvas.scale(scale, scale)`

2. **`ViewStyle`** computes independent scales: `scaleX = width / displayWidth`, `scaleY = height / displayHeight`

3. **`CanvasModel`** stores and restores `_scaleX`/`_scaleY` alongside `_scale` in `reset()` and `clear()`

4. **`input_model.dart`** maps mouse coordinates with independent X/Y scales (also fixes a pre-existing bug where the uniform scale step was applied to both axes)

5. **Linux HiDPI**: stretch scale is divided by `displays[i].scale` for Linux peers

6. **Mobile pinch-zoom** is disabled in stretch mode (stretch fills the screen; zoom would conflict)

## Trade-offs

- **Aspect ratio distortion**: The image will be stretched to fill the window, so circles may become ovals if aspect ratios differ significantly. This is by design -- same as "stretch" mode in AnyDesk, TeamViewer, and Windows RDP.
- This is **complementary** to the existing Resolution menu. Stretch is client-side; Resolution change is host-side.

## Test plan

- [ ] Connect to a remote with different aspect ratio than local window
- [ ] Select "Scale stretch" from view style menu
- [ ] Verify no black bars, image fills entire window
- [ ] Verify mouse input maps correctly to remote coordinates
- [ ] Verify cursor position is correct in stretch mode
- [ ] Verify stretch mode persists across session reconnect
- [ ] Verify window resize recalculates stretch scales
- [ ] Verify Linux peer with HiDPI displays correctly

Generated with [Devin](https://devin.ai)

I followed the instructions from the website to install the docker container, and it is running correctly. title: [Bug] Wayland session with Xwayland but no XAUTHORITY enters 240-pipeline retry loop
author: dougvk
body:
### Bug Description

On an active `systemd-logind` Wayland session with Xwayland present, `rustdesk --service` selects `get_display_xauth_xwayland()`. On compositors that launch Xwayland from inherited listen file descriptors and do not export `XAUTHORITY` (observed with Hyprland), this path can never satisfy its `DISPLAY && XAUTHORITY` success condition even though `DISPLAY`, `WAYLAND_DISPLAY`, and `DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS` are all available.

Each failed refresh performs:

- 10 retry rounds
- 6 process patterns (`xdg-desktop-portal`, `Xwayland`, `ibus-daemon`, `goa-daemon`, `kded`, and the RustDesk tray)
- 4 environment variables per process

That is up to **240 calls to the legacy `get_env()` per refresh**. Every call starts a large `ps | grep | tail | awk | xargs cat | tr | grep | tail | sed` shell pipeline. The failed refresh takes several seconds, then the outer service loop repeats it indefinitely.

On the affected host, an idle 10-second sample consumed 9.31 CPU-seconds (93.1% of one core). The long-lived service/server/tray processes together used only about 0.6%; nearly all CPU time belonged to the short-lived discovery children. No remote client connection is needed to trigger the behavior.

This is not a request to remove the baseline `pgrep`/`loginctl` polling discussed in #11959. It is a narrower branch-selection failure: logind reports a Wayland session, but the mere presence of Xwayland routes `Desktop::refresh()` through an X11-authentication path that cannot succeed on this environment. #15520 reported the broader CPU/process-spawning symptom, but did not identify this trigger or validate the existing pure-Wayland branch as a correction. Unlike the baseline polling overhead, this failure path becomes an effectively continuous fork storm.

#### Relevant source paths on current `master`

- Legacy shell pipeline: [`get_env()`](https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/blob/137298e05a10ebac2b12c933b7efebc7c217adec/src/platform/linux.rs#L1375-L1378)
- 10 × 6 × 4 retry loop and `DISPLAY && XAUTHORITY` gate: [`get_display_xauth_xwayland()`](https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/blob/137298e05a10ebac2b12c933b7efebc7c217adec/src/platform/linux.rs#L1686-L1707)
- Wayland session routed into the Xwayland path whenever `pgrep -a Xwayland` succeeds: [`Desktop::refresh()`](https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/blob/137298e05a10ebac2b12c933b7efebc7c217adec/src/platform/linux.rs#L1905-L1942)
- Existing pure-Wayland path already uses native `/proc` discovery and accepts `WAYLAND_DISPLAY && DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS`: [`get_display_xauth_wayland()`](https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/blob/137298e05a10ebac2b12c933b7efebc7c217adec/src/platform/linux.rs#L1655-L1684)

The relevant processes had these keys (values intentionally omitted):

```text
Xwayland:           DISPLAY, WAYLAND_DISPLAY, DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS
xdg-desktop-portal: DISPLAY, WAYLAND_DISPLAY, DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS
rustdesk --tray:    DISPLAY, WAYLAND_DISPLAY, DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS
XAUTHORITY:         absent from all of the above
```

### How to Reproduce

1. Log in to a Wayland session with Xwayland enabled. Hyprland is a known reproducer.
2. Confirm that logind reports Wayland, Xwayland is running, and Xwayland has no `XAUTHORITY` key:

   ```bash
   session_id=$(loginctl list-sessions --no-legend | awk -v uid="$UID" '$2 == uid && $4 == "seat0" { print $1; exit }')
   loginctl show-session "$session_id" -p Type -p State -p Seat

   xwayland_pid=$(pgrep -u "$UID" -x Xwayland | head -n 1)
   pgrep -a Xwayland
   tr '\0' '\n' < "/proc/$xwayland_pid/environ" \
     | sed -n 's/=.*//p' \
     | grep -E '^(DISPLAY|XAUTHORITY|WAYLAND_DISPLAY|DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS)$'
   ```

   Expected environment-key output for the affected case:

   ```text
   WAYLAND_DISPLAY
   DISPLAY
   DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS
   ```

3. Start the system service and let startup settle:

   ```bash
   sudo systemctl restart rustdesk.service
   sleep 15
   ```

4. Measure the service cgroup for 10 seconds:

   ```bash
   unit=rustdesk.service
   cpu0=$(systemctl show "$unit" -p CPUUsageNSec --value)
   time0=$(date +%s%N)
   sleep 10
   cpu1=$(systemctl show "$unit" -p CPUUsageNSec --value)
   time1=$(date +%s%N)

   awk -v c0="$cpu0" -v c1="$cpu1" -v t0="$time0" -v t1="$time1" \
     'BEGIN { printf "RustDesk service CPU: %.1f%% of one core\n", 100 * (c1-c0) / (t1-t0) }'
   ```

5. Optionally confirm the exec loop with `strace` (values such as the numeric UID may appear locally, but need not be included in a report):

   ```bash
   service_pid=$(systemctl show rustdesk.service -p MainPID --value)
   sudo timeout 15 strace -f -e trace=execve -s 300 -p "$service_pid" 2>&1 \
     | grep -E 'pgrep -a Xwayland|ps -u [0-9]+ -f.*(DISPLAY|XAUTHORITY|WAYLAND_DISPLAY|DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS)' \
     | head -n 30
   ```

The result is a continuous stream of the `get_env()` shell pipeline, especially searches for the absent `XAUTHORITY` key.

### Expected Behavior

- A logind Wayland session should use Wayland environment discovery even when Xwayland is available for compatibility applications.
- `XAUTHORITY` should be optional when `WAYLAND_DISPLAY` and the session D-Bus address are valid.
- The idle root service should not continually spawn large shell pipelines.
- Successfully discovered session state should be cached until the active session changes, or retries should use bounded backoff.
- Native X11 session discovery should continue to require whatever X11 authentication state it actually needs.

### Operating system(s) on local (controlling) side and remote (controlled) side

No controlling client is required -> Arch Linux (kernel 7.0.9), Hyprland 0.55.2, Xwayland 24.1.11, xdg-desktop-portal-hyprland 1.3.12

### RustDesk Version(s) on local (controlling) side and remote (controlled) side

No controlling client is required -> RustDesk 1.4.8. The same code path and legacy loop are still present in 1.4.9 and current `master` at commit `137298e05a10ebac2b12c933b7efebc7c217adec`.

### Screenshots

Not applicable. The CPU measurement and text-only process trace above provide the reproduction evidence.

### Additional Context

#### Proposed fix

The smallest candidate correction is to prioritize the known session protocol over the presence of a compatibility Xwayland process:

```rust
// Conceptual change in Desktop::refresh():
if self.is_wayland() {
    self.get_display_xauth_wayland();
} else if is_xwayland_running() {
    self.get_display_xauth_xwayland();
}
```

Apply the same ordering both when initially discovering the seat-0 session and when refreshing an already active session. `get_display_xauth_wayland()` already collects optional `DISPLAY`/`XAUTHORITY` values when available, while correctly using `WAYLAND_DISPLAY && DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS` as the success condition. This behavior was validated by forcing RustDesk's existing pure-Wayland branch, rather than by testing a compiled source patch. The source change should therefore also be checked on GNOME and KDE Wayland sessions with Xwayland enabled.

A more conservative alternative would be to migrate `get_display_xauth_xwayland()` from four separate legacy `get_env()` shell calls per candidate to the native `get_envs()` `/proc` implementation, and use this success condition when `self.is_wayland()`:

```rust
has_wayland_display && has_session_bus
```

In that case `DISPLAY` and `XAUTHORITY` can remain optional compatibility values. Successful results should be cached until the logind session ID/state changes; failures should use backoff rather than immediately repeating the full scan.

#### Validation of the branch change

As a diagnostic workaround, only the root RustDesk supervisor was made to receive a negative result for the exact `pgrep -a Xwayland` probe. Xwayland remained enabled globally and the user-owned RustDesk server retained normal process detection.

That forced the existing pure-Wayland branch without changing any capture or input code:

- The environment-search pipelines disappeared completely.
- Idle service usage fell from 93.1% to 7.9% of one core.
- Wayland portal capture, hardware H.264 encoding, and uinput keyboard/mouse initialization continued to work.
- A direct remote desktop session completed successfully.

The remaining approximately 8% is the broader `loginctl`/`ps aux` polling already discussed in #11959; it is separate from the 240-pipeline failure documented here.

#### Suggested regression coverage

1. Wayland session + Xwayland running + no `XAUTHORITY`: select the Wayland discovery path and do not call legacy `get_env()`.
2. Wayland session with optional `DISPLAY`/`XAUTHORITY`: retain those values without making them the success condition.
3. Native X11 session: preserve existing X11 discovery/authentication behavior.
4. Failed discovery: verify bounded retry/backoff and no unbounded shell-process churn.

Related reports: #15520 and discussion #11959.

title: Korean (Hangul) soft-keyboard composition still broken on Android controller — root cause identified in #15429, please prioritize (#7727 follow-up)
author: realitsyourman
body:
### Bug Description

Typing Korean (Hangul) from an Android controller to a Windows host still breaks jamo composition: only the first jamo of each syllable arrives at the host.

- `안녕` arrives as `ㅇㄴ`
- `만나` arrives as `ㅁ나`

Medial vowels and final consonants (받침) are silently dropped. This is the same defect as #7727, which has been open since April 2024 and makes RustDesk effectively unusable for Korean-speaking users controlling a desktop from Android.

I am filing this follow-up because the root cause has now been pinpointed (analysis and reference fix in PR #15429), and in that PR the maintainers said this core input path must be fixed by the core team itself. **I would like to respectfully ask that this be prioritized** — the root-cause analysis and a tested reference implementation are already available.

Root cause summary (full details in #15429): `_handleNonIOSSoftKeyboardInput` in `flutter/lib/mobile/pages/remote_page.dart` diffs the hidden soft-keyboard text field by **string length** and no-ops when the length is unchanged. Korean/CJK IMEs mutate the composing syllable **in place** (ㅁ → 마 → 만), and each precomposed Hangul syllable is a single UTF-16 code unit, so these in-place mutations never reach the host. The iOS handler diffs by content and does not have this bug.

Keyboard mode: default soft-keyboard input on mobile (not a legacy/map/translate physical-keyboard issue).

### How to Reproduce

1. Use an Android phone (e.g. Samsung Galaxy, with Samsung Keyboard or Gboard, Korean layout) as the controlling side and connect to a Windows host.
2. Focus any text input on the remote host.
3. Type `안녕` or `만나` with the soft keyboard.
4. Observe `ㅇㄴ` / `ㅁ나` arriving at the host.

### Expected Behavior

Composed Hangul syllables should arrive intact on the host (`안녕`, `만나`).

### Operating system(s) on local (controlling) side and remote (controlled) side

Android (Galaxy) -> Windows 10/11

### RustDesk Version(s) on local (controlling) side and remote (controlled) side

Latest release on both sides — reproducible continuously since it was first reported in #7727 (April 2024).

### Screenshots

Please see the reproduction screenshots/videos already attached to #7727; behavior is unchanged.

### Additional Context

- #7727 has been open for over two years; earlier attempts (e.g. #9769) touched a different path (physical-key handling in `input_model.dart`) and did not fix the soft-keyboard composing path.
- PR #15429 contains a content-diff fix (backspace-the-changed-tail then retype, same mechanism as the existing iOS handler) with 18 pure unit tests, offered as a reference per the maintainer's comment that the core team must handle this path carefully.
- I am happy to test dev builds on real Galaxy hardware (Samsung Korean QWERTY and 천지인/Chunjiin 12-key layouts) — please ping me anytime.

title: Linux/X11 host black screen after upgrade to 1.4.9; screen returns only after opening Chrome Remote Desktop, then display size/DPI becomes wrong
author: MatthewZMD
body:
### Bug Description

RustDesk had been working normally on this Linux VM for more than 6 months. I rarely use Chrome Remote
  Desktop; I only opened it today because RustDesk suddenly showed a completely black screen.

  After the issue started:

  - Connecting via RustDesk shows a black screen.
  - If I open/connect through Google Chrome Remote Desktop, the existing RustDesk session starts showing the
  desktop again.
  - If I turn off/close the Google Remote Desktop session/host interaction, the RustDesk session goes black
  again.
  - When Chrome Remote Desktop reconnects, it resizes the virtual display to the browser/client size, which
  makes the RustDesk view appear extremely zoomed/cluttered.
  - Keyboard/mouse/session appear alive, but the framebuffer/display capture becomes black or dependent on
  CRD activity.

  This began after RustDesk was upgraded from `1.4.6` to `1.4.9`.

  ## Timeline

  From apt/dpkg logs:

  ```text
  2026-07-07 15:45:36 apt install -y /tmp/rustdesk-update/rustdesk-1.4.9-x86_64.deb
  2026-07-07 15:45:37 upgrade rustdesk:amd64 1.4.6 1.4.9
  2026-07-07 15:45:40 status installed rustdesk:amd64 1.4.9

  RustDesk was working before this upgrade on 1.4.6.

  ## Environment

  Controlled/host machine:

  OS: Debian GNU/Linux 13 (trixie)
  Kernel: Linux 6.12.86+deb13-cloud-amd64
  Desktop: XFCE
  Session type: X11
  RustDesk: 1.4.9
  Previous working RustDesk: 1.4.6
  Chrome Remote Desktop: 146.0.7680.5
  Display used by RustDesk: DISPLAY=:20

  Relevant installed packages:

  rustdesk 1.4.9
  chrome-remote-desktop 146.0.7680.5
  xfce4-session 4.20.2-2
  xorg 1:7.7+24+deb13u1
  xserver-xorg-core 2:21.1.16-1.3+deb13u3
  xserver-xorg-video-dummy 1:0.4.0-1

  RustDesk server process environment:

  DISPLAY=:20
  XAUTHORITY=/home/<user>/.Xauthority
  XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/run/user/1000

  Chrome Remote Desktop is also running an Xorg dummy display:

  /usr/lib/xorg/Xorg :20 -auth /home/<user>/.Xauthority -nolisten tcp -noreset ...

  ## Display Details

  Normal/intended virtual desktop baseline:

  dimensions: 2912x1832
  X server DPI: 96x96
  XFCE /Xft/DPI: 144

  When Chrome Remote Desktop reconnects, it may resize the display to the browser/client size, for example:

  1216x868 [96, 96]

  CRD log example:

  Choosing best candidate for client resolution: 1216x868 [96, 96]
  Resizing monitor ID ... to 1216x868 [96, 96]
  Resizing RANDR Output ... to 1216x868

  After that, RustDesk becomes visually wrong/zoomed/cluttered.

  ## Logs

  RustDesk repeatedly logs connection-manager/window teardown errors after the issue starts:

  flutter: --cm started
  flutter: model  closed
  gtk_widget_set_opacity: assertion 'GTK_IS_WIDGET (widget)' failed
  gtk_window_get_position: assertion 'GTK_IS_WINDOW (window)' failed
  gtk_window_get_size: assertion 'GTK_IS_WINDOW (window)' failed
  gtk_widget_hide: assertion 'GTK_IS_WIDGET (widget)' failed
  gtk_window_move: assertion 'GTK_IS_WINDOW (window)' failed
  gtk_window_resize: assertion 'GTK_IS_WINDOW (window)' failed
  Attempted to set message handler on an FlBinaryMessenger without an engine
  Attempted to set message handler on an FlBinaryMessenger without an engine
  Attempted to set message handler on an FlBinaryMessenger without an engine

  Also present, but likely not fatal because it appears in other working RustDesk logs:

  Cannot load libcuda.so.1
  Cannot load libcuda.so.1


### How to Reproduce

See above

### Expected Behavior

RustDesk should continue capturing and displaying the Linux/X11 desktop independently, as it did on 1.4.6,
  without requiring Chrome Remote Desktop to be opened.

  Chrome Remote Desktop connecting or disconnecting should not make RustDesk go black.

  ## Actual Behavior

  RustDesk shows a black screen until Chrome Remote Desktop is opened. Once CRD opens, RustDesk starts
  showing the desktop again. If CRD interaction stops, RustDesk can go black again.

  CRD also changes the virtual display size, which makes RustDesk’s view look heavily zoomed/cluttered.

  ## Notes

  This may be related to display/monitor restore behavior introduced in 1.4.9, especially:

  Feature: Restore the last viewed monitor on auto reconnect #15441

  The timing strongly points to a regression between 1.4.6 and 1.4.9.

  I have not yet downgraded, but 1.4.6 was the last known working version on this machine.

  ## Workaround

  Opening Chrome Remote Desktop temporarily makes RustDesk display the desktop again, but this is not a real
  workaround because CRD can resize the display and cause scaling/layout problems.

### Operating system(s) on local (controlling) side and remote (controlled) side

MacOS -> Debian

### RustDesk Version(s) on local (controlling) side and remote (controlled) side

1.4.9 -> 1.4.9

### Screenshots

N/A, black screen when it happens

### Additional Context

_No response_

title: Rustdesk always creates an autostart entry
author: bercedev
body: ### Bug Description Every time the system starts up, a new tray icon appears, and there’s no way to hide it in the app settings! I really enjoy using it, but to keep PlasmaShell clean, I hide every app from there. It’s surprising that an issue from 2023 still exists today. [#4863](https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/issues/4863) ### How to Reproduce Just make sure the “Open at system startup” setting in Rustdesk is enabled—that's all you need to do. ### Expected Behavior Just as every application can be hidden in KDE, RustDesk should also be able to be hidden. ### Operating system(s) on local (controlling) side and remote (controlled) side Cachyos Kde ### RustDesk Version(s) on local (controlling) side and remote (controlled) side 1.4.9 ### Screenshots ### Additional Context _No response_

title: Double downloading breaks key in filename
author: mdsipl
body: ### Bug Description Windows adds (1) (2) ... (100) in filename when double downloading. It broke last parameter (key or host). ### How to Reproduce Double download rustdesk client with host/key declaration in filename. Run second file. ### Expected Behavior Please dont copy that string from filename to key/host field in Rustdesk. ### Operating system(s) on local (controlling) side and remote (controlled) side Windows 11 ### RustDesk Version(s) on local (controlling) side and remote (controlled) side newest ### Screenshots ### Additional Context _No response_

title: Wayland/KDE Plasma 6: host transmits vertically-flipped (180°) screen to all clients on multi-GPU (amdgpu) host; OBS captures the same portal correctly
author: iosmand
body:
### Bug Description

On a Linux **KDE Plasma 6 Wayland** host, RustDesk captures and transmits the screen **vertically flipped** (the received image is inverted on both axes = rotated 180°: the top panel appears at the bottom, text reads mirrored). This happens on the **capture/host side**, not the viewer: the flipped image is sent to *every* client.

Key evidence that this is a RustDesk capture bug and **not** a compositor / GPU / display-configuration problem:

- **The physical host display is correct.** `kscreen-doctor` reports `Rotation: 1` (normal) with an identity transform, and the desktop is displayed correctly on the local monitor.
- **OBS Studio captures correctly.** OBS uses the *same* `xdg-desktop-portal` PipeWire ScreenCast path on the *same* GPU and shows the desktop upright. This strongly suggests RustDesk's PipeWire consumer is **ignoring the buffer's vertical-flip / transform metadata** (the `SPA_META_VideoTransform` / dmabuf y-invert flag) that OBS honors.
- **Two independent clients both show the flip** (iOS and Android RustDesk), confirming the host is sending flipped frames rather than a viewer-side rendering bug.

This appeared after enabling **VFIO GPU passthrough**: the primary NVIDIA GPU was bound to `vfio-pci`, so the KDE desktop now renders on the secondary **AMD iGPU (amdgpu)**. Before passthrough (desktop on the NVIDIA GPU) capture was upright. So the trigger seems to be the **multi-GPU / amdgpu dmabuf capture path** where the vertical-flip flag is not applied.

### How to Reproduce

1. Multi-GPU Linux host, KDE Plasma 6 **Wayland** session, desktop rendered by an **AMD iGPU (amdgpu)** (a second GPU is bound to `vfio-pci` for passthrough).
2. Run the RustDesk service and connect to this host from any client (tested: iOS and Android).
3. Observe the remote screen: it is vertically flipped / rotated 180° (top panel at bottom, mirrored text).
4. For comparison, capture the same host with OBS Studio via the KDE screen-cast portal → the image is upright.

### Expected Behavior

RustDesk should transmit the screen in the correct orientation, honoring the PipeWire/dmabuf buffer transform (vertical-flip) flag exactly as OBS does, so the remote view matches the physical display.

### Operating system(s) on local (controlling) side and remote (controlled) side

iOS / Android (controlling) -> CachyOS (Arch-based), Linux 7.1.3-1-cachyos, KDE Plasma 6.7.2 / KWin 6.7.2 **Wayland**, Mesa 26.1.4, desktop GPU = AMD Ryzen 7 9700X integrated GPU (amdgpu), discrete NVIDIA GPU bound to `vfio-pci` for passthrough (controlled)

### RustDesk Version(s) on local (controlling) side and remote (controlled) side

iOS (latest, App Store) / Android (latest) -> 1.4.8 nightly build (also reproduced on 1.4.8-1 stable, Arch `rustdesk-bin`)

### Screenshots

Screenshot from the iOS client:

![RustDesk iOS client showing the remote desktop rotated 180 degrees / vertically flipped](https://i.ibb.co/4wbsx4gr/IMG-1413.png)

The entire remote desktop is rendered **rotated 180° / vertically flipped**:

- The KDE **top panel** runs along the top edge but its icons and text are **upside-down and mirrored**.
- The desktop wallpaper is inverted (the foreground object hangs down from the top instead of sitting at the bottom).
- A KDE **System Monitor** widget in the lower-right shows mirrored, upside-down text and sensor readings.

Crucially, RustDesk's own overlays are rendered **upright** by the client: the connection HUD in the top-right (`Speed / FPS / Delay / Target Bitrate / Codec H264 / Chroma 4:2:0`) and the bottom toolbar are correct. Only the **captured/transmitted desktop stream** is flipped, which localizes the bug to the host-side capture rather than the client renderer.

For reference, capturing the same host with **OBS Studio** via the same KDE screen-cast portal renders the desktop **upright**.

### Additional Context

Things already tried on the host that did **not** fix it (documenting to save triage time):

- Disabling hardware codec (`enable-hwcodec = 'N'`) and forcing **VP9** from the client → still flipped (rules out the NVENC/encoder path).
- Updating to the **nightly** master build (1.4.8-0) → still flipped (no fix in current master).
- Forcing the Wayland/portal path via systemd env (`WAYLAND_DISPLAY`, unset `DISPLAY`) → RustDesk still logs `DISPLAY=Ok(":1")` and injects its own DISPLAY.
- Attempting to force the memfd/SHM buffer path by breaking EGL (`__EGL_VENDOR_LIBRARY_FILENAMES`, `LIBGL_ALWAYS_SOFTWARE`, `MESA_LOADER_DRIVER_OVERRIDE`) → still flipped (never black), so the flip also affects the non-dmabuf path or EGL still initialized.
- **Refreshing the ScreenCast permission**: cleared `wayland-restore-token` in `RustDesk_local.toml` and deleted the portal permission store (`~/.local/share/flatpak/db/screencast`), then re-granted the KDE screen-share prompt on reconnect → brand-new token, fresh negotiation, **still flipped** (rules out a stale restore token / stale output binding from before the passthrough change).

Log line confirming the capture path selection:
```
INFO [src/server.rs:582] DISPLAY=Ok(":1")
```

The reproducer is reliable and OBS provides a clean reference implementation of the same portal capture rendered upright, so this should be fixable by applying the buffer's vertical-flip / transform flag in RustDesk's PipeWire capture consumer.

title: [Bug] Black screen when controlling macOS from Windows (Only mouse cursor visible)
author: Chen-xu-Wang
body: ### Bug Description Description: I am experiencing a display issue when using RustDesk to control a macOS device from a Windows machine. While controlling the Windows machine from macOS works perfectly, connecting from Windows to macOS results in a black screen. Expected Behavior: The remote desktop screen of the macOS host should be visible and interactive after a successful connection. Actual Behavior: The connection is established, but the screen remains entirely black. Notably, the mouse cursor is visible and follows my movements, but the desktop background, icons, menu bar, and application windows are completely invisible. Environment: Controller (Client): Windows [Windows11] Controlled (Host): macOS [15.7.7] RustDesk Version: [1.4.8] Troubleshooting Steps Taken: I have thoroughly verified the macOS System Settings. All necessary permissions, including Accessibility, Screen Recording, and Full Disk Access, have been granted to RustDesk in System Settings > Privacy & Security. I have restarted both the RustDesk service and the macOS device, but the issue persists. ### How to Reproduce I am experiencing a display issue when using RustDesk to control a macOS device from a Windows machine. While controlling the Windows machine from macOS works perfectly, connecting from Windows to macOS results in a black screen. ### Expected Behavior The remote desktop screen of the macOS host should be visible and interactive after a successful connection. ### Operating system(s) on local (controlling) side and remote (controlled) side Windows11——>MacOS15.7.7 ### RustDesk Version(s) on local (controlling) side and remote (controlled) side 1.4.8->1.4.8 ### Screenshots ### Additional Context _No response_

title: [Bug] Linux service causes severe CPU spikes by aggressively polling "ps" and reading "/proc/PID/environ" to find display variables
author: oguzkarayemis
body:
### Bug Description

My AI assistant and I were investigating a strange behavior on my Pop!_OS (Ubuntu-based) machine where a mysterious process was causing a 200% CPU spike for a few seconds every 30-40 seconds. 

Since the process lived and died in milliseconds, standard monitoring tools (like `top` or `execsnoop`) couldn't catch it easily. We eventually set up a booby trap (a wrapper script) over the `/usr/bin/ps` binary to log exactly who was calling it and with what arguments. 

The trap successfully caught the culprit: `rustdesk --service` (PID 1379).

It turns out that the RustDesk background service is executing a massive shell chain multiple times every few seconds to find active graphical sessions. It spams commands like this:

`/bin/sh -c ps -u 1000 -f | grep -E 'xdg-desktop-portal' | grep -v 'grep' | tail -1 | awk '{print $2}' | xargs -I__ cat /proc/__/environ 2>/dev/null | tr '\0' '\n' | grep '^DISPLAY=' | tail -1 | sed 's/DISPLAY=//g'`

It repeats this exact pipeline for `Xwayland`, `ibus-daemon`, `goa-daemon`, `kded`, and `rustdesk --tray` to search for variables like `DISPLAY`, `WAYLAND_DISPLAY`, `XAUTHORITY`, and `DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS`.

**The Issue:** Polling the entire process list (`ps -u 1000 -f`), grepping, and reading `/proc/PID/environ` dozens of times every few seconds is incredibly inefficient. It leads to severe CPU spikes, heats up the machine, and drains battery on laptops. 

There should be a more elegant, event-driven way (or at least a significantly less aggressive polling interval) to detect the current user's display environment variables without brute-forcing the `ps` command in a loop.

### How to Reproduce

1. Install RustDesk on a Linux system (Tested on Pop!_OS / Ubuntu).
2. Ensure the background service is running (`sudo systemctl start rustdesk`).
3. Leave the system idle and monitor CPU usage. You will notice CPU spikes every 30-40 seconds.
4. To see the exact behavior, you can use a tool like `fatrace` (`sudo fatrace | grep ps`) or replace `/usr/bin/ps` with a logging wrapper script.
5. The logs will show `rustdesk --service` constantly triggering `/bin/sh -c ps ...` pipelines to read environment variables.

### Expected Behavior

The RustDesk background service should remain lightweight and idle, consuming near-zero CPU when a remote connection is not actively being initiated. 

To detect user session variables (`DISPLAY`, `WAYLAND_DISPLAY`, `XAUTHORITY`, etc.):
1. The service should ideally use native system APIs or event-driven mechanisms (like `systemd-logind`, `dbus` signals, or Wayland/X11 socket monitoring) to track active graphical sessions, rather than aggressively polling the process tree.
2. If polling is strictly necessary as a fallback mechanism, the service should cache these variables once they are found and only re-check them when a session change is detected, or at a significantly reduced interval (e.g., every few minutes instead of every few seconds).
3. Under no circumstances should the background service trigger massive shell pipelines (`ps | grep | awk | xargs cat`) in a continuous loop, causing CPU spikes and thermal throttling on the host machine.

### Operating system(s) on local (controlling) side and remote (controlled) side

Pop!_OS 24.04

### RustDesk Version(s) on local (controlling) side and remote (controlled) side

1.4.8

### Screenshots

No video or screenshot.

### Additional Context

_No response_

title: Mouse Default Position Causing Remote Control Issues
author: novemberei
body:
### Bug Description

When remotely controlling a macOS computer, the mouse defaults to appearing in the top-left corner. This can cause the top-left corner shortcuts to be triggered directly during remote control, potentially resulting in the screen being automatically turned off or locked. And if the screen turned off, it can not be activated from the remote side, which cause control failure. It is recommended to change the default mouse position to the center of the screen.

### How to Reproduce

1. Use RustDesk to connect the macOS 26 remote computer from the Windows 10 master station.
2. Observed the mouse automatically appearing in the upper left corner of a macOS computer.
3. Due to improper mouse position settings, accidental activation of the quick-access keys in the upper left corner occurred during remote control, resulting in the display being turned off or locked.

### Expected Behavior

The mouse default position should be set in the middle of the screen to avoid accidental shortcut key presses and ensure normal use of remote control.

### Operating system(s) on local (controlling) side and remote (controlled) side

win11 to osx 26.5.1

### RustDesk Version(s) on local (controlling) side and remote (controlled) side

1.4.8 to 1.4.8

### Screenshots

BLACK SCREEN

### Additional Context

_No response_

title: Can't middle click or click drag at all on remote blender3D on wayland
author: jurassicjordan
body:
### Bug Description

I'm trying to work on a blender project remotely, in order to take advantage of my remote machine's specs, but attempting to drag or middle click drag to rotate the viewport, or even scrolling to zoom, etc locks the cursor in place til mouse key is released. this only occurs on blender 3D running on wayland. please fix this issue.

both remote and client are flatpak versions of rustdesk

### How to Reproduce

connect to remote machine running blender on wayland
attempt to rotate viewport with middle click
cursor stays in place for no reason

### Expected Behavior

when rotating viewport with middle click, it is expected that the viewport rotates, something is blocking this interactionbetween rustdesk, wayland and blender

### Operating system(s) on local (controlling) side and remote (controlled) side

Linux Bazzite -> Linux EndeavourOS

### RustDesk Version(s) on local (controlling) side and remote (controlled) side

1.3.2 -> 1.4.8

### Screenshots

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/21026135-5c1c-4722-8b1b-18efc69c6fd9

### Additional Context

_No response_

title: Android controlling windows keyboard inconsistent behaviour since 1.4.6
author: harrickeyh
body: ### Bug Description After 1.4.6, controlling windows through android is inconsistent. Typing works fine (letters show up properly), but using it in applications with keyboard shortcuts would not work properly. For example, in one of my windowed games, pressing any letters would not trigger anything, unless used with a modifier key. I tested this at https://www.keyboardtester.com/tester.html as well, and it seems that all keystrokes sent through the android client doesn't show up as key presses, whereas before 1.4.6, it works well. ### How to Reproduce 1. Connect to a windows client 2. Try to control some game inputs / use keyboard tester 3. Press some keys on your android keyboard 4. See that nothing is showing up on the keyboard keypress, even though the letters are coming through. ### Expected Behavior 1. Key presses are registered in game / keyboard tester. ### Operating system(s) on local (controlling) side and remote (controlled) side Android -> Windows 11 ### RustDesk Version(s) on local (controlling) side and remote (controlled) side (1.3.x ~ 1.4.x) -> 1.4.6+ ### Screenshots Typing "hello world" from android. See that only the modifier keys are lit up (green border) Typing "hello world" from android pre 1.4.6 (on windows side). Letters are lit up as well as modifiers. (green border) Sending keystroke post 1.4.6 with modifier key -- example CTRL, then pressing C). Letters are lit up (which in this case the "c" keystroke would also go through to the windowed game) ### Additional Context Reverting the windows client to pre 1.4.6 allows my keystrokes to be accepted properly and passed on to the apps / keyboard tester app properly. I believe the actual key stroke is what the apps are listening for, which at the absence of, wouldn't trigger app shortcuts / buttons.

title: File copy stops at 2GiB
author: brandishwar
body:
### Bug Description

Rustdesk cuts off file copies at 2GiB in size. File copy will progress but suddenly cut off at 2GiB.

### How to Reproduce

1. Identify a file greater than 2GiB (2,147,483,648 bytes) in size - e.g., a Linux distro ISO
2. Select the file in the remote system's file manager
3. Paste the file into the client's file manager. File copy will progress but stop suddenly.
4. Open the file properties. File size is exactly 2,147,483,648 bytes (2 GiB).

### Expected Behavior

100% of a file larger than 2GiB is copied successfully.

### Operating system(s) on local (controlling) side and remote (controlled) side

Windows 11 -> EndeavorOS (Linux)

### RustDesk Version(s) on local (controlling) side and remote (controlled) side

1.4.8 -> 1.4.8

### Screenshots

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/cca81f17-1f51-4861-aced-9d1ef76f03a5

### Additional Context

_No response_

title: Relative mouse mode: Left and right mouse buttons cannot be pressed simultaneously
author: Ami-OS
body: ### Bug Description When relative mouse mode is enabled, the left and right mouse buttons cannot be pressed simultaneously. ### How to Reproduce Turn on the relative mouse mode, then try pressing the left and right mouse buttons simultaneously. Can test it through the following website: https://www.onlinemictest.com/mouse-test/ ### Expected Behavior The left and right buttons can be triggered simultaneously. ### Operating system(s) on local (controlling) side and remote (controlled) side Windows 11 -> Windows 11 ### RustDesk Version(s) on local (controlling) side and remote (controlled) side 1.4.8 -> 1.4.8 ### Screenshots Normal mode: Relative mouse mode (Cannot be pressed at the same time): ### Additional Context _No response_
